title: MD
biography: Daniel Hale Williams was an African-American surgeon who performed one of the first successful open-heart surgeries in 1893, repairing a stab wound to the pericardium. He founded Provident Hospital in Chicago, the first non-segregated hospital in the United States and one of the first to have a nursing school for African Americans.
aiSummary: Daniel Hale Williams was a surgery pioneer who performed one of the first successful open-heart operations in 1893. Founded the first non-segregated hospital in the US. Based in Chicago, United States.
awards: Charter member, American College of Surgeons

timeline.title: Founded Provident Hospital
timeline.description: Established the first non-segregated hospital in the US with the first African American nursing school

timeline.title: Performed one of first successful open-heart surgeries
timeline.description: Successfully repaired the pericardium of James Cornish after a stab wound to the chest

timeline.title: Appointed Chief Surgeon at Freedmen's Hospital
timeline.description: Reorganized and modernized the historically Black hospital in Washington, DC

timeline.title: Charter member of American College of Surgeons
timeline.description: Only African American among the founding members
